name: Estimating Limit Values from Graphs
description: read a limit value directly from a graph

name: Estimating Limit Values from Tables
description: read a limit value from a numeric table

name: Determining Limits Using Algebraic Properties
description: factor, rationalize, or substitute to evaluate a limit algebraically

name: Types of Discontinuities
description: classify or identify removable, jump, or infinite discontinuities

name: Defining Continuity at a Point and over an Interval
description: apply the continuity definition

name: Infinite Limits and Vertical Asymptotes
description: evaluate a limit that goes to +/-inf as x approaches a

name: Limits at Infinity and Horizontal Asymptotes
description: find end behavior by comparing degrees or using dominant terms

name: Intermediate Value Theorem
description: invoke IVT to guarantee existence of a value on an interval

name: Trig Limits from Squeeze Theorem
description: Applying sinx/x

name: Average Rate of Change
description: compute the slope of a secant line or estimate a derivative numerically

name: Defining the Derivative; Derivative Notation
description: use the limit definition of the derivative

name: Differentiability
description: applying differentiability definition

name: Power Rule
description: differentiate x^n

name: Constant, Sum, Difference, Constant Multiple Rules
description: combine basic derivative rules linearly

name: Derivatives of trig functions, e^x, lnx
description: apply standard transcendental derivative formulas

name: Product Rule
description: differentiate a product of two functions

name: Quotient Rule
description: differentiate a ratio of two functions

name: Chain Rule
description: differentiate a composite function

name: Implicit Differentiation
description: differentiate an equation in x and y implicitly to find dy/dx

name: Differentiating Inverse Functions
description: apply the (f inverse) prime formula

name: Differentiating Inverse Trig Functions
description: differentiate arcsin, arccos, arctan, etc.

name: Higher-Order Derivatives
description: compute f double prime, f triple prime, or higher

name: Interpreting the Derivative in Context
description: state units or real-world meaning of f prime in an applied scenario

name: Straight-Line Motion Differentiation
description: relate position, velocity, and acceleration using derivatives

name: Related Rates
description: differentiate an equation relating two changing quantities

name: Local Linearity and Linearization
description: build or use a tangent-line approximation L(x)

name: L Hopital Rule
description: resolve a 0/0 or inf/inf indeterminate form by differentiating numerator and denominator

name: Mean Value Theorem
description: invoke MVT to guarantee existence of c where f prime(c) equals the average rate

name: Optimization
description: find an absolute max or min in an applied context using calculus

name: EVT, Critical Points
description: identify critical points; apply EVT on a closed interval

name: Increasing/Decreasing Intervals
description: use the sign of f prime to determine where f rises or falls

name: First Derivative Test and Local Min/Max
description: classify relative extrema by the sign change of f prime at a critical point

name: Candidates Test
description: find absolute extrema on a closed interval by comparing critical point and endpoint values

name: Concavity
description: use the sign of f double prime to determine concave up/down; locate inflection points

name: Second Derivative Test
description: classify a critical point as relative max/min using the sign of f double prime

name: Sketching Graphs of f and f prime
description: connect graph features to derivative behavior

name: Riemann Sum Approximations
description: estimate area using left, right, midpoint, or trapezoidal sums

name: Riemann Sums, Summation Notation, Definite Integral Notation
description: set up or interpret sigma or integral notation

name: FTC Part 1 Accumulation Functions
description: differentiate g(x) = integral from a to x of f(t) dt, using chain rule if needed

name: Interpreting Accumulation Functions
description: describe behavior of F(x) = integral of f in terms of net area or rate

name: FTC Part 2 Evaluating Definite Integrals
description: compute integral from a to b of f dx = F(b) minus F(a)

name: Antiderivatives and Indefinite Integrals
description: apply basic antiderivative rules; include +C

name: U-Substitution
description: use u-substitution to evaluate an integral

name: Verifying Solutions to Differential Equations
description: substitute a function into a DE to confirm it satisfies the equation

name: Slope Fields
description: read or match a slope field to a differential equation

name: Separation of Variables General Solution
description: separate dy and dx; integrate both sides; solve for y

name: Separation of Variables Particular Solution
description: apply an initial condition after separating variables

name: Exponential Growth/Decay Models
description: solve dy/dt = ky; interpret k and the model in context

name: Average Value of a Function
description: compute (1/(b-a)) times integral from a to b of f dx

name: Position/Velocity/Acceleration via Integrals
description: recover position or displacement by integrating velocity

name: Accumulation in Applied Contexts
description: compute net change or total amount as a definite integral in a real scenario

name: Area Between Curves Functions of x
description: integrate |f(x) - g(x)| with respect to x

name: Area Between Curves Functions of y
description: integrate with respect to y

name: Volumes with Cross-Sections
description: integrate A(x) or A(y) for a known cross-sectional shape

name: Disk Method Around x or y Axis
description: integrate pi times [f(x)] squared

name: Disk Method Around Other Axes
description: shift the radius when revolving around a non-coordinate axis

name: Washer Method Around x or y Axis
description: integrate pi times ([f(x)] squared minus [g(x)] squared)

name: Washer Method Around Other Axes
description: adjust inner and outer radii for a non-coordinate axis
